seoTitle: Do Adjusting Entries in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to record and flag adjusting journal entries in QuickBooks Online for period-end closes, using the Is Adjusting Journal Entry toggle.
intro: Closing out an accounting period often means writing off accrued liabilities or updating depreciation, and QuickBooks Online has a specific way to flag these as adjusting entries rather than routine transactions. This guide covers posting a balanced entry and using the accountant-facing tag to separate it out for reporting.

steps.heading: Start a journal entry
steps.body: Click + New, then select Journal Entry under the Other column.

steps.heading: Set your closing date
steps.body: In the upper right form field, set the official reporting period closing date.

steps.heading: Flag it as an adjusting entry
steps.body: Accountant users can toggle the Is Adjusting Journal Entry? checkbox to tag the entry for auditing.

steps.heading: Enter your balanced lines
steps.body: Input the debit value on the first account line, then choose the offsetting account and let QuickBooks auto-populate the matching credit.

steps.heading: Add a memo, verify, and save
steps.body: Write a clear explanation in the memo box, confirm the totals balance to zero, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: What does the Is Adjusting Journal Entry toggle actually do?
faq.answer: It tags the entry so it can be isolated later in accountant-exclusive reports like the Adjusted Trial Balance, separate from routine transactions.

faq.question: Is this toggle available to all users?
faq.answer: This feature is described as available to accountant-tier users specifically.

faq.question: How do I view all my adjusting entries after posting them?
faq.answer: Run a Journal report from the Reports panel and filter by transaction type to isolate adjusting entries.

faq.question: What happens if my debits and credits don't balance?
faq.answer: The entry won't save correctly until the totals reach zero, so double-check the amounts before clicking Save and Close.
